channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitics headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇳🇿 US Approves $1.5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
item.description: The US State Department approved the sale of five MH-60R Seahawk maritime helicopters to New Zealand, supporting Wellington's plans to nearly double military spending amid growing regional security concerns.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo to Meet Abuse Survivors During Spain Visit
item.description: Pope Leo will meet with survivors of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy during his week-long visit to Spain, his first to an EU country outside Italy, where he will also inaugurate a new tower at Barcelona's Sagrada Familia basilica.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Markets Rise Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Dubai's stock market outperformed regional peers even as hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict faded, with investors appearing to price in limited escalation risks.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Trump's 'Crazy' Rebuke Undercuts Netanyahu at Critical Moment
item.description: President Trump's public criticism of Prime Minister Netanyahu over Gaza policy has exposed a rift at a crucial time, complicating Israel's position as it navigates ceasefires with both Iran and Hezbollah.

item.title: 📉 European Shares Slip as Tech Rally Stalls, Middle East Weighs
item.description: European stocks ended the week lower as technology stocks paused their two-month rally and Middle East uncertainty kept investors cautious, with major indices posting weekly losses.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Attacks Iranian Coastal Sites After Iran Launches Drones
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the US military said, in the latest escalation complicating efforts to end the war between the two countries.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest Swap
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel in the latest prisoner of war swap, officials in both countries said, continuing limited humanitarian cooperation amid ongoing hostilities.

item.title: 🇷🇴 Marine Drone Self-Detonates in Romanian Black Sea Port
item.description: A sea drone self-destructed near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties, as Ukraine said Russia jammed the vessel causing it to drift off course.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Drone Attack Kills Four in Kyiv Region
item.description: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in Ukraine's Kyiv region killed four people, officials said, as Moscow continued aerial assaults on Ukrainian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Bank of Canada Expected to Hold Rates Through 2026
item.description: The Bank of Canada is expected to keep interest rates steady through 2026, looking past temporary inflation pressures as the economy navigates uncertainty from global trade tensions.

item.title: 🇦🇲 What's at Stake in Armenia's Election on Sunday
item.description: Armenia holds parliamentary elections on Sunday that pit the governing Civil Contract party, pursuing closer Western ties, against pro-Russian opposition parties, determining the country's geopolitical direction.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Macron, Starmer and Merz to Meet Zelenskiy in London on June 7
item.description: French President Macron, British PM Starmer and German Chancellor Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Zelenskiy on Sunday, the Elysee said, as European leaders coordinate support for Kyiv.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Says No Reason to Meet Zelenskiy Currently
item.description: Russian President Putin said he currently sees no reason to meet with Ukraine's Zelenskiy, rebuffing calls for face-to-face talks as fighting continues.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Declares Support for Hezbollah as Peace Deal Doubts Grow
item.description: Iranian authorities publicly expressed support for Hezbollah, complicating ceasefire negotiations and raising questions about the viability of broader peace agreements in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Five Dead in Russian Attacks on Southern Kherson Region
item.description: Russian attacks killed five people in Ukraine's southern Kherson region in three separate incidents, the regional governor said, as Moscow continues assaults on Ukrainian territories.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Heads to Spain with Migrants and Polarisation in Focus
item.description: Pope Leo set off for a week-long visit to Spain, his first to an EU country outside Italy, where he will inaugurate a new tower in Barcelona's Sagrada Familia and meet migrants who braved dangerous Atlantic waters.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Held Friendly Meeting with Germany's Schroeder, Kremlin Says
item.description: Russian President Putin held a friendly one-on-one meeting with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, the Kremlin said, amid ongoing tensions between Moscow and the West.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK House Prices Fell Unexpectedly in May, Halifax Says
item.description: UK house prices fell unexpectedly in May, according to Halifax data, surprising analysts who had expected modest growth in the housing market.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Adds 87,800 Jobs as Jobless Rate Falls to 6.6%
item.description: Canada added 87,800 jobs in May, beating estimates, with the unemployment rate falling to 6.6% as the labor market showed resilience despite economic headwinds.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran World Cup Players Granted US Visas, White House Says
item.description: Iran's World Cup soccer players have been granted visas to enter the United States, a White House official said, just 10 days before their first match in Los Angeles amid ongoing conflict between the two countries.

item.title: ⛽ Companies to Add 40M Barrels to US Strategic Reserve After Iran War
item.description: Companies that borrowed oil from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve will add back an extra 40 million barrels as premiums after the Iran conflict ends, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright said.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Be Handed to Ukraine
item.description: A Swedish court ruled that the seizure of a cargo vessel in the Baltic Sea was lawful and that the ship can be handed to Ukraine, where it is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

item.title: 🇦🇿 Russia Accuses Ukraine of Killing Azerbaijani Crew in Drone Attack
item.description: Russia accused Ukraine of killing Azerbaijani crew on board two cargo vessels headed for a Russian port in a drone attack which Baku said had killed at least five of its citizens.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC Output Plunges as US Squeezes Iran, Survey Shows
item.description: OPEC oil production dropped further as US pressure on Iran reduced the country's output, a survey showed, tightening global supply amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.
